chief_complaint: A 47-year-old male presented with fever and abdominal pain.
history: The patient had a 3-day history of fever, abdominal pain, and thrombocytopenia. He had no significant past medical history and was not taking any medications. He was from an endemic region for scrub typhus.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, pulse 110 bpm, respiratory rate 24/min. Physical examination revealed abdominal tenderness and guarding.
labs: Elevated pancreatic enzymes (amylase 500 U/L, lipase 2000 U/L), thrombocytopenia (platelet count 80 x 10^9/L), and characteristic imaging findings of acute pancreatitis.
